We'll evaluate on the piece itself: stamps, serial numbers, construction, stones, metal. How do you verify designer jewelry authenticity? We look at hallmarks, serial numbers, how it's made, weight, and stones — not just "it feels real." If we can't verify something represented as designer, we won't pretend we can. We'd rather be honest than buy a question mark. Do you buy worn or outdated designer jewelry? Often yes — wear happens.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Heavy damage hurts more than light scratches, but a loved Tiffany bracelet isn't automatically dead. We'll quote what today's market actually pays for it. Can I sell jewelry received as a gift? We've seen every version of that story. No judgment — just an offer. Discreet, quick, and you never have to explain the backstory if you don't want to. Do you buy designer watches? Depends on the brand and model — some cross into our watch buyer lane. Bring it in; if it's better handled on our watches page, we'll say so. Otherwise we'll fold it into the jewelry evaluation. Why might a signed piece not receive an offer? Common reasons: can't verify authenticity, heavy damage, or the secondhand market for that exact piece is soft right now. We'll tell you why — and you're still welcome to sell just the metal/stones if that's where the value is.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Do you buy Tiffany silver and gold? Both walk in — Return to Tiffany silver bracelets, gold pieces, diamonds in Tiffany mountings. We price metal and stones separately from the brand premium when the piece is right. What if I only have copies of papers? Still helpful for context. We care most about the physical piece — stamps, serial numbers, construction. Papers support the story; they're not always required to make an offer. Can I sell multiple rings from one visit? We can evaluate one or both. No judgment, no story required. Discreet, in-and-out — same as any other sale. Questions & Answers

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Real answers about selling gold, diamonds, silver, estate jewelry and designer pieces in NYC. Browse by topic below. Getting Started What kind of merchandise do you buy? At East Village Buyers we buy gold, silver, platinum, diamonds, colored gemstones, sterling flatware, coins, estate and vintage jewelry, and signed designer pieces when authenticity checks out. See the What We Buy sections above for the full list. How much can you buy? There is no real limit — whether it is one gold earring or a full estate collection, we evaluate and buy based on what you have. For very large lots, text photos to 917-608-8939 ahead so we can set aside enough time. How do appointments in NYC work? Call or text 917-608-8939 to schedule time with our appraiser. We are at 39 Avenue A in the East Village, open Sunday–Thursday 12:30–6:30 and Friday 12:30–6.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Walk-ins are welcome; for big estate collections, giving us a heads-up helps us reserve dedicated time. Do I need an appointment, or can I just walk in? Walk-ins are welcome during store hours — most people just show up with a bag. If you want dedicated time without waiting while we assist other customers, texting ahead for a larger lot is a smart move. When & how will I get paid? We always offer a price on the spot after the appraisal. Same-day cash is what most people choose. For larger purchases, check is available — just tell us what works at the counter. Is it safe to sell my valuables to East Village Buyers? Yes. East Village Buyers operates as Vintage USA Inc with NYC Department of Consumer Affairs license #2070477. We have a real storefront at 39 Avenue A — not a mail-order operation or anonymous kiosk. You watch testing and weighing at the counter before you decide anything.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Do you offer a mail-in kit? We focus on in-person appraisals so you can watch testing and weighing at the counter — no shipping, no revised offers by email a week later. Most customers prefer seeing the process and leaving with cash the same day. Text photos to 917-608-8939 if you want a rough idea before you visit. What if I can't visit the store in person? The appraisal happens in our shop so you can see everything tested in front of you. If you are visiting NYC before flying home, plan a walk-in during store hours with valid ID — passport works. Send high-quality photos and a description first so we can advise whether a visit is worth the trip. Can I sell to East Village Buyers from another country? We primarily serve walk-in customers in NYC, but visitors from abroad sell to us regularly before flying home.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Send photos and details to 917-608-8939 along with any certifications or appraisals. Different countries have different customs rules, so we will coordinate if shipping is involved. How long does the appraisal process take? Generally 15–30 minutes depending on how many pieces you bring. A large jewelry box or estate lot may take 45 minutes to an hour — we will not rush you out the door. What should I bring with me to the appraisal? Bring your jewelry plus valid government-issued photo ID — New York law requires it. Appraisals, GIA certificates, receipts, or original boxes help but are not required. Bring everything you want evaluated; there is no limit on items. How are offers calculated? Based on weight, metal purity, today's spot prices, diamond quality (the 4 Cs), supply and demand, condition, and brand when verified.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: For diamonds, a GIA or AGS certificate helps the appraiser make a stronger offer. The evaluation is free — you only sell if you accept. Is your offer negotiable? We aim to be fair from the start and walk you through the math at the counter. If you have questions about the number, ask — we are happy to explain. No pressure to sell on the spot; take your time and compare elsewhere if you want. Will you have to remove the stones from my setting during an appraisal? Almost never. We evaluate metal and stones in the setting. If removal were ever necessary — very rare — we would ask permission first and could reset the stone without taking it out of your sight. Location & Reviews How is East Village Buyers reviewed around the web? Check us on Google, Yelp, and social — we are a neighborhood fixture on Avenue A with real walk-in customers.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Ask in the shop if you want references; we have built our business on transparent counter-side testing and same-day payouts. What is the best way to get to you? 39 Avenue A, New York, NY 10009 — East Village, between 2nd and 3rd Streets. L train to 1st Ave, 6 train to Astor Place, plenty of buses. Street parking can be tight; subway is easiest. See us on Google Maps for turn-by-turn directions. What are your store hours? Sunday–Thursday 12:30–6:30 · Friday 12:30–6. Holidays can shift things — text or call if you are making a special trip and want to double-check. Do customers come from outside the East Village? All the time — Brooklyn, Queens, New Jersey, Midtown office workers on lunch, students, families handling estates. No neighborhood requirement; just bring your items and valid photo ID.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Gold & Precious Metals How is gold jewelry priced? Roughly: weight × purity × today's spot, minus a fair margin so we can keep the lights on. A 14K stamp is a hint — we still test. Spot changes daily, so the honest answer is "come weigh it." Anyone giving you a firm number blind is guessing. What types of gold do you buy? Yep — jewelry, scrap, coins, dental gold, the works. Higher karat = more gold per gram, but a fat 10K chain still adds up. Don't leave it in a drawer because it's "only 10K." Do you buy broken gold jewelry and single earrings? Really. We buy them every day. Metal doesn't care if the clasp is broken. That one earring? Bring it. Do you buy dental gold? Seriously. Crowns, bridges, sweeps. Shake out obvious gunk if you can; we'll weigh and test the rest. Weird to sell, normal to us.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Do you buy gold coins and bars? Yes — Eagles, Buffalos, Maples, Krugerrands, Philharmonics, lots of bars and world coins. Packaging is nice if you have it, not required. What is the difference between gold-plated and gold-filled? Plated fashion stuff — no. Gold-filled sometimes yes when the stamp shows real weight (like 1/10 14K GF). HGE, GP, gold tone — usually a no. Bring it and we'll tell you in two minutes. How do you test gold without a stamp? Yes. Old estate gold often has worn or missing stamps. We test the metal instead of guessing from how yellow it looks. Diamonds & Gemstones Can I sell my engagement ring here? Right here — 39 Avenue A. Bring the ring, any cert, and your ID. We'll grade it in person, explain the number, and keep it low-key. No appointment needed for most people. Do you buy diamonds without a certificate? All the time.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Certs are nice; lack of one isn't a dealbreaker. We grade in person — cut, color, clarity, carat — and tell you what we see. Do you buy chipped or damaged diamonds? Often still worth something. Chips and worn prongs matter, but they don't always kill the offer. Bring it; let us look before you assume it's done. Do you buy lab-grown diamonds? Yes — we'll ID them as lab-grown and price today's lab market, separate from natural stones. Not sure what you have? We'll tell you. Do you buy rubies, sapphires, and emeralds? Lots of fine colored stones in gold or platinum mountings work. Loose fashion stones with no real metal — usually not. Lab reports help on the expensive stuff. Silver, Platinum & Coins Do you buy sterling flatware and tea sets? Yes — real .925 flatware, trays, candlesticks, tea sets. We test because plate looks shiny too. EPNS isn't sterling money.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: What silver coins do you buy? Yup — Morgans, Peace dollars, Eagles, pre-1965 dimes/quarters/halves. Bring the jar. We'll sort it at the counter. Do you buy platinum wedding bands? Yes — PT950, PT900, bands with or without diamonds. We test; white gold and platinum aren't the same price. What types of sterling silver do you buy? Yes — .925 rings, chains, cuffs, tarnished or shiny. We test first, then weigh. What is junk silver and do you buy it? Yes — pre-1965 US silver, rolls, partial collections. Bring the whole tub. Do you buy inherited silverware? If it's real sterling, yes. We test because plate lives in those drawers too. Partial sets welcome. Estate, Designer & Inherited What is the difference between estate, vintage, and antique jewelry? Estate = previously owned, any age. Vintage = a style era (Art Deco, Retro, etc.). Antique = usually 100+ years.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: You don't need the perfect label — bring the box. Do you buy estate jewelry collections? Yes — one brooch or a whole house. Executors and families welcome. Text photos if it's huge so we're ready for you. Do you buy Tiffany and Cartier jewelry? When they're real and the market wants them, yes. Box and papers help; no box still okay. We verify — we don't just take someone's word because it "looks like" Cartier. What is a maker's mark? Little stamps or signatures that tell you who made it. Helpful for estate and designer pieces, but we still test metal and stones — a mark alone isn't proof. Do you buy David Yurman jewelry? When authentic and condition supports it, yes — David Yurman is a regular walk-in. Bring the piece; we'll verify stamps and construction. Do you buy Mikimoto and pearl jewelry? Fine pearl strands and signed pearl jewelry can qualify when pearls and clasps are real.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Fashion pearls — we'll tell you quickly. Do you buy unworn gift jewelry? Very normal. Tags, box, no box — we see unworn gifts weekly. No judgment, just an offer. Appraisal, Pricing & Payout How does the appraisal process work? We weigh it, test purity, look at any stones and mountings, then walk you through the offer before you decide anything. It's what we would actually pay to buy it not some inflated insurance number. And it's completely free. Is the appraisal free? Completely free. We only make money if you sell and only if you like the price. No appraisal fees at the end. How are offers calculated? For gold, silver and platinum it's weight times purity times today's spot. Diamonds are the 4Cs plus what the market is paying right now. Designer pieces factor in brand, authenticity, condition and the metal. That old $6000 appraisal from 1995 is a useful story but not today's number.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: How do I get the most for my jewelry? Bring everything because small pieces add up. Grab any certs or old paperwork if you have them. Check today's gold spot so you have some context going in. Get a second quote if you want. And don't aggressively polish anything that might be plated. When & how will I get paid? Same day cash is the usual deal when you accept. For really large lots we can do a check if that works better for you. Just let us know at the counter. Is your offer negotiable? Our number is our best number. We show the math at the counter. You're never stuck saying yes and a lot of people come to us after getting a lowball offer somewhere else. Why is my offer lower than retail price? Retail price includes markup, brand, rent and profit. We buy based on metal, stones and what the resale market actually looks like today. We'll explain the gap in a way that makes sense.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Is there a fee if I decline the offer? No fee at all. Walk out with your jewelry and nothing changes. We only make money when you sell. Can you provide a written offer? Ask at the counter. We're happy to break down weight, karat and price for you. Good for comparing offers or dealing with estate paperwork. Legal, ID & Safety Do I need ID to sell jewelry? Yes. New York law requires a valid government photo ID to sell precious metals to a licensed dealer. Driver's license, passport or state ID. Just something current with your photo. Are you licensed? Yes. East Village Buyers, DBA Vintage USA Inc, NYC DCA #2070477. Licensed precious metals and jewelry buyer. Happy to talk about it any time. Is it safe to sell in person vs. mailing? For most people yes. You watch the test, you get paid, you leave. No envelope anxiety, no revised offer email a week later.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: If you do mail anything anywhere use real insurance and a buyer with a solid reputation. Do you report sales to authorities? We follow NYC and state rules for licensed dealers. Records and reporting are part of the deal to catch stolen goods. Don't bring anything stolen. It wastes everyone's time and lands you in trouble. Gemstones & Colored Stones Do you buy rubies and sapphires in gold rings? When they're real stones in fine mountings, yes. We evaluate the metal and the stone — estate rings with rubies and diamonds are common here. Do you buy pearl necklaces? Often, when pearls and clasps test real and condition is sellable. Costume pearls on thread with base metal clasps — usually not. Do you buy birthstone jewelry? If the band is real gold and stones are real, often yes. Plastic stones in cheap metal — probably not. Bring them; sorting is free.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Do you buy cocktail rings with multiple stones? Bring them. We check if center or side stones are real and what the gold weighs. Some are mostly metal value; some surprise you. Quick Answers Do you buy broken gold chains? Yes — every day. Snapped, tangled, one link holding on for dear life. Priced by tested karat and weight. Do you buy single earrings? Yes — the mate is gone, the gold isn't. Don't toss lone studs or hoops. Can I sell an entire inherited jewelry box? Yes — whole box, no sorting required. We'll go through it with you and explain each offer. What condition does jewelry need to be in? Usually yes if there's real metal or real stones. A beat-up gold chain is still gold. Diamonds and designer names care more about condition, but "ugly" alone isn't a dealbreaker. Do you buy watches? Lots of gold and precious-metal watches, yes.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Fancy watch brands might be better on our watches page — bring it either way and we'll steer you right. What merchandise do you not buy? Costume with no real metal. Gold-plated mall jewelry. Silver plate pretending to be sterling. "Designer" we can't verify. Stolen goods. Loose junk stones with no fine mounting. When you're unsure, bring it — worst case we say no nicely. Do you buy class rings and religious jewelry? When there's gold content — class rings, crosses, medals, Stars of David, Italian gold charms. We test and weigh like everything else. Do you buy diamond tennis bracelets? Yes — diamond tennis bracelets in gold or platinum are common. Missing stones? Still worth a look. Do you buy pocket watches and vintage watches? Many gold cases and estate watches, yes. Bring the whole piece; we'll separate case value from movement when needed.

chunks.text: How It Works Sell in One Visit Most folks are in and out in about 15–30 minutes. Checking what you've got is completely free — you only sell if the number feels right. Same friendly process whether you're holding one gold chain or your mom's entire jewelry box.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Gold We Buy & Sell Gold in NYC 10K–24K gold, broken chains, single earrings, dental gold, and gold watches. Gold's gold — whether it's a Cuban link, a class ring, or a tangled knot of chains from a drawer that hasn't been opened in twenty years. We buy it every day, priced off the live spot market (the same number you'd Google if you searched cash for gold NYC ). You'll see the weight and karat on our scale before you decide anything. No mail-in kit that "revised" your offer, no guessing from across the counter. What types of gold do you buy? Pretty much all of it. 10K, 14K, 18K, 22K, 24K. Yellow, white, rose gold. European stamps like 585, 750 and 916 too. Higher karat means more gold per gram but even a heavy 10K chain adds up more than people expect.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Diamonds We Buy & Sell Diamonds in NYC Engagement rings, wedding bands, loose stones, and estate diamonds. Engagement rings, loose stones, grandma's old mine cut — we look at diamonds in person, right here on Avenue A. Got a GIA or IGI cert? Great, bring it. No cert? That's normal too. If the ring says Tiffany or Cartier on the inside and we can back up the authenticity, the mounting can add real value on top of the stone. Do you buy diamonds without a certificate? All the time. A cert makes things easier but plenty of good stones never had one. We grade it in person and explain what we see. Ask as many questions as you want.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Silver We Buy & Sell Silver in NYC Sterling rings, chains, cuffs, scrap, and tarnished pieces. Sterling jewelry — rings, chains, cuffs, that chunky bracelet you never wear — we weigh and test it right at the counter. Tarnished? Bent? Still fine. Got flatware or coins instead? Jump to Silverware or Coins below. If you Googled sell sterling silver NYC , you're in the right neighborhood — literally and figuratively. What types of sterling silver do you buy? Mostly .925. Rings, chains, cuffs, brooches, body jewelry. Mexican silver and artisan pieces that test at .925 or higher too. If it passes the test we weigh it.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Silverware We Buy & Sell Sterling Silverware in NYC Flatware, tea sets, trays, and partial sets — real .925 only. That inherited flatware drawer? You're not alone — it's one of the most common reasons people walk through our door. Full sets, half sets, three random forks and a gravy ladle — we test for real .925 sterling (not plate), weigh it, and explain the offer before you sell anything. Silver plate and EPNS won't pass as sterling, and we'll tell you honestly if that's what you have. How do you verify sterling flatware? We test everything. Sterling or .925 stamps are a clue but we don't just take them at their word. A lot of shiny wedding gift stuff is plate not sterling. Better to find out at the counter than assume.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Coins We Buy & Sell Gold & Silver Coins in NYC Eagles, Morgans, junk silver, bars, and inherited collections. Dad's coin jar, a tube of Eagles, a shoebox from an estate — we buy common U.S. and world gold and silver coins, plus plenty of bars and rounds. Pricing starts with metal content and what the market's actually paying that day. You searched sell gold coins NYC or junk silver near me ? Cool. Bring them in, we'll test and weigh at the counter, and you leave with cash if you like the offer. What gold coins do you buy? American Gold Eagles, Buffalos, Maple Leafs, Krugerrands, Philharmonics, Liberty gold, most world gold coins and bars from major refiners. Mint packaging is nice but you don't need it.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Platinum We Buy & Sell Platinum in NYC Platinum bands, chains, scrap, and watch cases. Platinum wedding bands, heavy engagement rings, watch cases — when it's real, it's worth real money. Platinum's denser than gold, so a ring that looks small can still weigh a lot. We test PT950, PT900, and PLAT-marked pieces on site, diamonds included, and we never assume from color alone (white gold and platinum are not the same thing). What types of platinum do you buy? Wedding and engagement bands, comfort-fit men's bands, chains, bracelets, earrings, studs, scrap, and bench filings — basically anything marked PT950, PT900, PLAT, or testing as platinum.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Estate We Buy & Sell Estate Jewelry in NYC Full jewelry boxes, probate lots, and single heirlooms. Estate just means previously owned — inheritance, downsizing, "I found this in Mom's dresser." Antique usually means roughly 100+ years old. Vintage is more about era — Art Deco, Retro, mid-century. You don't need to label everything before you walk in; bring the box and we'll sort it together. One ring or a whole collection — we've got you. Families, executors, and attorneys come through all the time. We keep it discreet and respectful because we know it's not just stuff. Can I sell an entire jewelry box at once? Absolutely. Mixed lots are our bread and butter — gold next to costume, diamonds next to silver plate we'll sort out. One visit, one conversation. If it's a big estate, text photos first so we can block out enough time.

chunks.pageTitle: We Buy & Sell Jewelry NYC | Gold, Diamonds, Silver, Platinum & Estate
chunks.text: Gemstones We Buy & Sell Colored Gemstones in NYC Rubies, sapphires, emeralds, pearls, and birthstone rings in real gold mountings. Ruby, sapphire, emerald, tanzanite, pearls in a gold clasp — if it's set in real gold or platinum, there's a good chance we can make an offer. Loose stones work too when quality and today's market support it. Got a GIA or AGL report? Bring it. Old appraisal from 1987? That helps us understand the story even if the dollar amount isn't today's number. We Buy Rubies We Buy Sapphires We Buy Emeralds We Buy Pearls

Broken, scratched, mismatched, vintage, or still in the mall bag — condition is rarely the dealbreaker. Metal and stones are. Still worth a look. We Buy Tiffany We Buy Cartier We Buy David Yurman
